_meta.note: Daemon-backed persistent session start (omni P1). A session is a long-lived login-shell PTY whose cwd/env stay sticky across shell_session_exec lines. Gated by PolicyAction::SessionStart behind the allow_session capability (default deny) and audited before spawn. Success returns bounded identifiers and the session state, never a raw shell stream.

unsupported_platform_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [UnsupportedPlatform]: persistent shell sessions are not available on this platform yet (PTY/ConPTY pending)

policy_denied_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [PolicyDenied]: shell_session_start denied: allow_session capability is off or profile forbids sessions

invariants: session start is denied by default (allow_session capability off) and surfaces a PolicyDenied error, never a synthetic success
every accepted start is audited with a redacted subject before the shell PTY is spawned
env is an ARRAY of {key,value} objects, not a map; bounded by MAX_SESSION_ENV_ITEMS
success payload contains only session_id, bucket_id, and state
session output is combed (read from the bucket via shell_session_exec), never a raw stream
max_sessions is enforced before spawn; over-cap returns a bounded SessionLimitExceeded error, never a hang
